title: Color Picker
description: Color Picker lets users enter or pick a color value for themes, brand tokens, charts, and visual customization settings. Use it when a plain text field is too error-prone and users benefit from a visual picker plus format-aware output.
intro: Color Picker builds on the shared field system, so it supports labels, helper and error text, validation hooks, disabled and readonly states, and Angular forms integration. It adds color-specific features such as HEX, RGB, and HSL output, alpha control, an optional eye dropper, a connected picker panel, and native color input fallback on mobile.

featureSections.title: Basic color input
featureSections.description: Use the field when users need a concrete token or accent color, not an arbitrary paint app. A clear label and a sensible default usually matter more than exposing every picker option immediately.

featureSections.title: Output formats
featureSections.description: Choose the format that best matches the consuming system. HEX is common for design tokens, while RGB and HSL are often easier to manipulate in code or theme tooling.

featureSections.title: Variants and density
featureSections.description: The color field follows the shared input variants and sizes, so it can sit naturally inside forms, compact toolbars, or denser settings panels without inventing a separate visual language.

featureSections.title: Alpha and picker tools
featureSections.description: Turn on alpha when transparency is part of the product model, and hide features like the eye dropper when they would add noise or unsupported expectations in a given workflow.

featureSections.title: Brand token form
featureSections.description: A practical color picker usually lives in a form with several related tokens and a compact readout of the current values. This is more useful than showing isolated pickers alone.

featureSections.title: Theme settings panel
featureSections.description: More advanced examples should combine multiple controls into a ready-to-adapt configuration surface. This pattern mixes color fields, transparency, and actions in a compact theme panel.

accessibility.title: Accessible name
accessibility.body:
The text trigger input uses the shared field accessible name chain, so `ariaLabel` overrides the visible label and the label remains the default naming source. When there is no visible label, provide an explicit accessible name.

| Source | Accessible name |
| --- | --- |
| `ariaLabel` | explicit accessible name |
| visible `label` | default fallback |
| format preview | supplemental only, not the name |

accessibility.title: Descriptions and errors
accessibility.body:
Helper text and error text are wired into `aria-describedby` through the shared field wrapper. The trigger also exposes `aria-expanded`, `aria-haspopup="dialog"`, and `aria-controls` when the custom picker panel is used.

| Trigger state | Attribute |
| --- | --- |
| helper or error text | `aria-describedby` |
| open picker | `aria-expanded="true"` |
| connected panel | `aria-controls` |
| picker relationship | `aria-haspopup="dialog"` |

accessibility.title: Picker panel
accessibility.body: The overlay picker renders as a dialog and traps focus while open. Keep it as a lightweight editing surface for the current field value rather than a complex multi-step workflow.

apiSections.title: ColorComponent - ui-color
apiSections.lead: Standalone color input built on the shared FieldComponent. It integrates with Angular forms through ControlValueAccessor and adds a connected picker panel, format-aware value serialization, alpha control, and optional eye dropper support.

blocks.title: Inputs
rows: Controls how the current color is serialized into the field value.
Shows opacity controls and includes alpha in serialized output when below 1.
Shows the eye dropper action when supported by the browser.
On viewports up to 768px, uses the native color input inside the same framed swatch as desktop (border, padding, square) next to the text field.
Width of the connected picker panel in pixels.
Optional override for the unsupported eye dropper warning message.

blocks.title: Inherited field inputs
rows: Shared field surface token inherited from `FieldComponent`.
Controls where the field label is placed relative to the control.
Supporting helper text rendered below the field when there is no active error.
Validation or manual error message managed by the shared field base.
Disables the field and its interactive affordances.
Keeps the value visible while preventing user changes.
HTML form name used by the field wrapper and native control wiring.
Control id used to connect label, help text, and error messaging.
Inherited autocomplete hint for native form compatibility.
Inherited shared-field clear-button flag when supported by the concrete control.
Overrides the accessible name when no visible label is appropriate.
Appends extra descriptive ids to the computed `aria-describedby` value.
Enables inherited automatic validation error handling from `FieldComponent`.

blocks.title: Outputs
rows: Emitted when the serialized color value changes.

paragraphs: The component implements ControlValueAccessor, so it works with ngModel, reactive forms, and Angular validators.
Calling clear() resets the serialized value to an empty string and restores the internal picker state to black with full opacity.
blocks.title: Notes
Related mechanisms
items: FieldComponent - shared base handling labels, helper and error text, aria wiring, and validation synchronization.
Angular CDK Overlay - powers the connected color picker panel.
ActionButtonComponent - renders the picker, eye dropper, and clear affordances.
